# DispatchSEO

> Open-source SEO manager built for Claude Code and MCP agents. The agent that
> already knows your product does keyword research, writes guides and free
> tools, and ships them as pull requests; DispatchSEO is the backend it works
> against - the suggestions queue, rank tracking, Search Console stats,
> scheduling, and a human approval dashboard. Self-hosted with one Docker
> command on your own machine, AGPL-3.0.

Key facts:
- The MCP server lives at /api/mcp (streamable HTTP, bearer token per project).
  It exposes state - suggestions, keywords, pages, rankings, GSC stats,
  backlink prospects - plus a get_instructions tool that serves the agent
  playbook. Research and content generation happen in the agent, not here.
- One deployment manages many sites; the bearer token selects the site.
- Data tiers stack: free (Google Search Console + autocomplete), free SerpApi
  key (live SERP checks), optional DataForSEO (volumes/difficulty).
- Content ships as pull requests to the site's own GitHub repo, with human
  approval by default (fully automatic mode is optional).

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/NeoZi12/dispatchseo#readme): what it is, the one-command install, quickstart
- [Self-hosting guide](https://github.com/NeoZi12/dispatchseo/blob/main/docs/SELF_HOSTING.md): full setup walkthrough
- [Agent setup skill](https://github.com/NeoZi12/dispatchseo/blob/main/SKILL.md): how an agent connects a site
- [Architecture notes](https://github.com/NeoZi12/dispatchseo/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): conventions, tenant model, MCP parity rule
